```asp
% 3×3 block selection: highest colour, then smallest row, then smallest column.
% Output the whole block (including zeros). Exactly one block must be chosen.

% ----------------- 1. Grid dimensions -----------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% ----------------- 2. Uniform 3×3 block --------------------
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C > 0,
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), color(C), C > 0.

% ----------------- 3. Highest colour in a uniform block -----
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% ----------------- 4. Smallest row for that colour ----------
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) }.

% ----------------- 5. Smallest column for that row ----------
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% ----------------- 6. The uniquely selected block ------------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% ----------------- 7. Output the nine cells -----------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    I = 0..2,
    J = 0..2,
    input(R,C,V).

% ----------------- 8. Constraints ---------------------------
% exactly nine output atoms (the whole block)
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% exactly one selected block (ensures uniqueness)
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

```asp
% 3x3 block selection: highest colour, then smallest row, then smallest column.
% Output the whole block (including zeros). Exactly one block must be chosen.

% ---------- 1. Grid dimensions ----------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% ---------- 2. Uniform 3x3 block of colour C ----------
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C > 0,
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), color(C), C > 0.

% ---------- 3. Highest colour appearing in a uniform block ----------
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% ---------- 4. Smallest row that still yields a uniform block ----------
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) }.

% ---------- 5. Smallest column for that row ----------
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% ---------- 6. The uniquely selected 3x3 block ----------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% ---------- 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block ----------
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + p,
    C = C0 + q,
    p = 0..2,
    q = 0..2,
    input(R,C,V).

% ---------- 8. Constraints ----------
% exactly nine output atoms (the whole block)
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% exactly one selected block (ensures uniqueness)
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

refinements.response:
```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection – highest colour, then smallest row,
% then smallest column.  Output the whole block (including zeros).
% Exactly one block must be selected.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- 1. Grid dimensions ----------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% start positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)  :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% ---------- 2. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C ----------
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C > 0,
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), color(C), C > 0.

% ---------- 3. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block ----------
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% ---------- 4. Smallest row for that colour ----------
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) }.

% ---------- 5. Smallest column for that row ----------
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% ---------- 6. The uniquely selected 3×3 block ----------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% ---------- 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block ----------
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + p,
    C = C0 + q,
    p = 0..2,
    q = 0..2,
    input(R,C,V).

% ---------- 8. Constraints ----------
% exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection – highest colour, then smallest row,
% then smallest column. Output the whole block (including zeros).
% Exactly one block must be selected.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)  :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0) .

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0), C > 0,
    CountOther = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    CountOther = 0,
    CountC = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    CountC > 0.

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 6. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 7. Positions inside the block
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2 .

% 8. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection – highest colour, then smallest row,
% then smallest column.  Output the whole block (including zeros).
% Exactly one block must be selected.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)  :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0) .

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0), C > 0,
    CountAll = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0
    },
    CountC = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    CountAll = CountC,
    CountC > 0.

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 6. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 7. Positions inside the block
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2 .

% 8. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

Thus final program:

% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    positive_color(C),
    CountAll = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), V != 0, ij(I,J) },
    CountC = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, C), ij(I,J) },
    CountAll = CountC,
    CountC > 0.

positive_color(C) :- C > 0.

ij(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), positive_color(C).

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 6. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 7. Positions inside the block (use same ij predicate)
ij_pos(I,J) :- ij(I,J).   % same definition, can be omitted and use ij directly

% 8. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    ij(I,J),   % reuse ij
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
9 { output(R,C,V) } :- true.

% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
1 { selected(R0,C0) } :- true.

% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)

col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection – highest colour, then smallest row,
% then smallest column.  Output the whole block (including zeros).
% Exactly one block must be selected.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)  :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0) .

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0), C > 0,
    CountAll = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0
    },
    CountC = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    CountAll = CountC,
    CountC > 0.

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 6. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 7. Positions inside the block
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2 .

% 8. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

```asp
% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    positive_color(C),
    CountAll = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), V != 0, ij(I,J) },
    CountC   = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, C),       ij(I,J) },
    CountAll = CountC,
    CountC   > 0.

positive_color(C) :- C > 0.

ij(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), positive_color(C).

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 6. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    ij(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
9 { output(R,C,V) } :- true.

% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
1 { selected(R0,C0) } :- true.
```

```
% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    CountAll = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), V > 0, ij(I,J) },
    CountC   = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, C), V > 0, ij(I,J) },
    CountAll = CountC,
    CountC > 0.

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 6. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    ij(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% 8. Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
output_total(N) :- N = #count { R,C,V : output(R,C,V) }.
:- output_total(9).

% 9. Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
selected_total(N) :- N = #count { R0,C0 : selected(R0,C0) }.
:- selected_total(1).

% auxiliary definitions
ij(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% 10. Compute the grid dimensions
#show output(R,C,V). % (optional)
```

max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% 3. Count all non‑zero cells in a 3×3 block
count_all(R0,C0,N) :- N = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), V > 0, ij(I,J) }.

% 4. Count cells of a specific colour C in a 3×3 block
count_c(R0,C0,C,N) :- N = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, C), V > 0, ij(I,J) }.

% 5. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%    – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    count_all(R0,C0,NAll),
    count_c(R0,C0,C,Nc),
    NAll = Nc,
    Nc > 0.

% 6. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% 7. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% 8. Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% 9. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    ij(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% 10. Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
output_total(N) :- N = #count { R,C,V : output(R,C,V) }.
:- output_total(9).

% 11. Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
selected_total(N) :- N = #count { R0,C0 : selected(R0,C0) }.
:- selected_total(1).

% auxiliary definitions
ij(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Transformation rule:
% The output is a 3×3 block whose non‑zero cells all have the same colour C.
% Among all such blocks we choose the one with the highest colour C;
% if several blocks have that colour we pick the smallest row (topmost)
% and, among those, the smallest column (leftmost).  The output consists
% exactly of the nine cells of that block, copied from the input.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Input dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
height(H)   :- max_row(MaxR),   H = MaxR + 1.
width(W)    :- max_col(MaxC),  W = MaxC + 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. All possible positions of a 3×3 block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row0(R0)  :- max_row(Mr), R0 = 0..Mr-3.
col0(C0)  :- max_col(Mc), C0 = 0..Mc-3.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row0(R0), col0(C0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Uniform 3×3 blocks (all non‑zero cells have the same colour C)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C != 0,
    Zero = #count {
        X : input(R0+i, C0+j, X),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        X != 0, X != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeastOne = #count {
        X : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    AtLeastOne > 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Helper predicate for uniform colours
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_color(Col) :- uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,Col), color(Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Highest colour C in a uniform block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_color(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { Col :
    uniform_color(Col), color(Col), Col != 0 }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Smallest row for Cmax
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :- R0 = #min { R' :
    uniform_subgrid(R', C0', Cmax) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Smallest column for that row
% --------------------------------------------------------------
col_best(C0) :- uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C' : uniform_subgrid(R0, C', Cmax) },
    row_best(R0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. The uniquely selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(C0), uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,Cmax).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Relative positions inside the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Output cells – copy the nine cells of the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J).

output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. Exactly nine output cells (no extra cells)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR)   :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC)   :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% top‑left corner of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row0(R0)   :- max_row(MaxR),  R0 = 0..MaxR-3 .
col0(C0)   :- max_col(MaxC),  C0 = 0..MaxC-3 .
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row0(R0), col0(C0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (all non‑zero cells are C,
%    at least one non‑zero cell exists)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C > 0,
    #count { X : input(R0+i, C0+j, X), i=0..2, j=0..2,
                X != 0, X != C } = 0,
    #count { X : input(R0+i, C0+j, C), i=0..2, j=0..2 } > 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Highest colour that occurs in a uniform block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_colour(Cmax) :-
    Cmax = #max { C : uniform_block(R0,C0,C), color(C), C > 0 }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Smallest row for the block with colour Cmax
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R' : uniform_block(R',C',Cmax) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Smallest column for that row
% --------------------------------------------------------------
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C' : uniform_block(R0,C',Cmax) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. The uniquely selected 3×3 block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0,Cmax) :-
    col_best(R0,C0),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Output the cells of the selected block that have colour Cmax
%    (these are exactly the non‑zero cells of the block)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0,Cmax),
    input(R,C,V),
    color(V), V > 0,
    V = Cmax.

% No extra constraints – the program will produce exactly one
% answer set (the block is uniquely determined).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection:
%   – all non‑zero cells in the block have the same colour C (C>0)
%   – at least one non‑zero cell exists
%   – among all such blocks choose the highest colour C,
%     then the smallest top‑left row, then the smallest top‑left column.
%   – output the whole 3×3 block (copying the nine cells from the input)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions (the input may be smaller than 3×3 in some
%    directions – we only consider positions that fit a 3×3 block)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

row0(R0)   :- max_row(MaxR), R0 = 0..MaxR-3 .
col0(C0)   :- max_col(MaxC), C0 = 0..MaxC-3 .
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row0(R0), col0(C0) .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. A uniform 3×3 block of colour C
%    – every non‑zero cell in the block has colour C
%    – at least one non‑zero cell exists
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C > 0,
    #count { R,C,V : input(R,C,V),
               R0 <= R <= R0+2, C0 <= C <= C0+2,
               V != 0, V != C } = 0,
    #count { R,C,V : input(R,C,V),
               R0 <= R <= R0+2, C0 <= C <= C0+2,
               V = C } > 0.

% Helper: any uniform block of colour C (ignoring the exact position)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), color(C), C > 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Highest colour C that appears in a uniform block
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
max_colour(Cmax) :-
    Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C), color(C), C > 0 } .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Smallest top‑left row that still yields a block of colour Cmax
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R' : uniform_block(R',C',Cmax) } .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Smallest top‑left column for that row
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C' : uniform_block(R0,C',Cmax) } .

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. The uniquely selected 3×3 block
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block (including zeros)
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2 .
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% exactly nine output atoms – no more, no less
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection – highest colour, then smallest row,
% then smallest column.  Output the whole block (including zeros).
% Exactly one block must be selected.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- 1. Grid dimensions ----------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% start positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)  :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% ---------- 2. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C ----------
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C > 0,
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), color(C), C > 0.

% ---------- 3. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block ----------
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% ---------- 4. Smallest row for that colour ----------
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) }.

% ---------- 5. Smallest column for that row ----------
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% ---------- 6. The uniquely selected 3×3 block ----------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% ---------- 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block ----------
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + p,
    C = C0 + q,
    p = 0..2,
    q = 0..2,
    input(R,C,V).

% ---------- 8. Constraints ----------
% exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

Let's propose final ASP program:

```
% grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% start positions of a 3x3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)   :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% Uniform 3x3 block of colour C (C>0)
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    N = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    N = 0,
    M = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    M > 0.

% candidate blocks for the chosen colour (max colour)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% smallest top-left row for that colour
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) }.

% smallest top-left column for that row and colour
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% The uniquely selected block
selected(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% Output the whole block (nine cells)
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% exactly nine output atoms (the block)
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
```

% grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% start positions for a 3×3 block
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% No non‑zero cell with a colour different from C
no_other(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    N = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    N = 0.

% At least one cell with colour C
has_C(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    M = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    M > 0.

% Uniform block of colour C
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :- subgrid(R0,C0), no_other(R0,C0,C), has_C(R0,C0,C).

% Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% Highest colour appearing in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% Smallest top‑left row for that colour
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) }.

% Smallest top‑left column for that row and colour
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% The uniquely selected block
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% Helper for positions inside the block
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% Output the whole block (copy the nine cells)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms (the whole block)
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
```

```
% grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% positions for a 3×3 block (must fit)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% No non‑zero cell different from C
no_other(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    N = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    N = 0.

% At least one cell equal to C
has_C(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    M = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    M > 0.

% Uniform 3×3 block of colour C
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :- subgrid(R0,C0), no_other(R0,C0,C), has_C(R0,C0,C).

% Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) } .

% Smallest top‑left row for that colour
row_best(R0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) } .

% Smallest top‑left column for that row and colour
col_best(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0),
    uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) } .

% The uniquely selected block
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% Positions inside the block
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% Output the nine cells of the selected block
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .

% Exactly one selected block
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

has_C(R0,C0,C) :- subgrid(R0,C0), C > 0,
    M = #count { V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C), i = 0..2, j = 0..2 },

uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% 8. The uniquely selected 3×3 block
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0).

% 9. Positions inside the block

% 10. Output the nine cells of the selected block
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms (the whole block)
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% Exactly one selected block
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3×3 block selection – highest colour, then smallest row,
% then smallest column.  Output the whole block (including zeros).
% Exactly one block must be selected.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) } .
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) } .

% 2. Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2 .
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2 .
subgrid(R0,C0)  :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0) .

% 3. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%   – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%   – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
no_other(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0), C > 0,
    N = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, V),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    N = 0.
has_C(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0), C > 0,
    M = #count {
        V : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    M > 0.
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :- subgrid(R0,C0), no_other(R0,C0,C), has_C(R0,C0,C) .

% 4. Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0 .

% 5. Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) } .

% 6. Smallest top‑left row for that colour
row_best(R0) :- max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) } .

% 7. Smallest top‑left column for that row and colour
col_best(R0,C0) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) } .

% 8. The uniquely selected 3×3 block
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(R0,C0) .

% 9. Positions inside the block
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2 .

% 10. Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) } .
```

refinements.program:
% Grid dimensions
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Positions of a 3×3 block (must fit inside the grid)
row_start(R0) :- max_row(MaxR), MaxR >= 2, R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col_start(C0) :- max_col(MaxC), MaxC >= 2, C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row_start(R0), col_start(C0).

% Count all non‑zero cells in a 3×3 block
count_all(R0,C0,N) :- N = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), V > 0, ij(I,J) }.

% Count cells of colour C in a 3×3 block
count_c(R0,C0,C,N) :- N = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, C), V > 0, ij(I,J) }.

% Uniform 3×3 block of colour C (C>0)
%   – all non‑zero cells in the block have colour C
%   – at least one non‑zero cell of colour C exists
uniform_block(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    C > 0,
    count_all(R0,C0,NAll),
    count_c(R0,C0,C,Nc),
    NAll = Nc,
    Nc > 0.

% Uniform block for any colour (positive)
uniform_any(C) :- uniform_block(R0,C0,C), C > 0.

% Highest colour that appears in a uniform block
max_colour(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_any(C) }.

% Smallest top‑left position for that colour
selected(R0,C0) :-
    max_colour(Cmax),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_block(R1, C1, Cmax) },
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_block(R0, C1, Cmax) }.

% Output the nine cells of the selected block (copy the block)
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    ij(I,J),
    input(R,C,V).

% Exactly nine output atoms – the whole block
output_total(N) :- N = #count { R,C,V : output(R,C,V) }.
:- output_total(9).

% Exactly one selected block – guarantees uniqueness
selected_total(N) :- N = #count { R0,C0 : selected(R0,C0) }.
:- selected_total(1).

% Enumerate cells of the 3×3 neighbourhood
ij(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Grid size
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) ) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Positions that fit a 3×3 block
%    (the block must stay inside the maximal coordinates)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row(R0)  :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) ),
            MaxR = #max { R2 : input(R2,_,_) ),
            R0 = 0..MaxR-2.                     % MaxR appears positively
col(C0)  :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) ),
            MaxC = #max { C2 : input(_,C2,_) ),
            C0 = 0..MaxC-2.                     % MaxC appears positively
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row(R0), col(C0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Uniform 3×3 block of colour C
%    – every non‑zero cell in the block equals C
%    – at least one cell equals C
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform(R0,C0,C) :-
    color(C), C>0,
    row(R0), col(C0),
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  “There exists a uniform block of colour C”
%    (C is the only variable in the head → safe)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_of_colour(C) :-
    uniform(R0,C0,C),                 % R0 and C0 are bound inside uniform
    row(R0), col(C0),
    color(C), C>0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Highest colour that occurs in a uniform block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
Cmax = #max { C : uniform_of_colour(C) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Smallest row (top‑most) that still gives a block of colour Cmax
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :-
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),          % Cmax already bound
    row(R0),                          % row(R0) gives a positive binding for R0
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), row(R1) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Smallest column (left‑most) for that row
% --------------------------------------------------------------
col_best(C0) :-
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),
    col(C0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), col(C1) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8.  The uniquely selected 3×3 block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0), col_best(C0), uniform(R0,C0,Cmax).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Output the nine cells of the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% non‑zero cell of the block
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    (input(R,C,V), V>0).

% implicit zero cell of the block
output(R,C,0) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    not input(R,C,_).

% exactly nine output atoms
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) }.

% exactly one selected block (guarantees uniqueness)
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) }.
```

We'll define:

```
% 1. Grid size
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% 2. Positions for 3x3 block (must be inside bounds)
row(R0) :- R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col(C0) :- C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row(R0), col(C0).

% 3. Uniform 3x3 block of colour C
uniform(R0,C0,C) :-
    color(C), C > 0,
    row(R0), col(C0),
    Zero = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), I = 0..2, J = 0..2, V != 0, V != C },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count { V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V), I = 0..2, J = 0..2, V = C },
    AtLeast > 0.

% 4. Uniform block of colour C exists
uniform_of_colour(C) :-
    uniform(R0,C0,C), row(R0), col(C0), color(C), C > 0.

% 5. Highest colour with a uniform block
cmax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_of_colour(C) }.

% 6. Top‑most row for Cmax
row_best(R0) :-
    cmax(Cmax),                     % bind Cmax
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),        % ensures there is a block of colour Cmax
    row(R0),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), row(R1) }.

% 7. Left‑most column for that row
col_best(C0) :-
    cmax(Cmax),
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),
    col(C0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), col(C1) }.

% 8. The selected block
selected(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0), col_best(C0), uniform(R0,C0,Cmax).

% 9. Helper for block coordinates
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% 9a. Output non‑zero cells
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V),
    V > 0.

% 9b. Output zero cells
output(R,C,0) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    not input(R,C,_).

% 9c. Exactly nine output atoms
9 { output(R,C,V) } :- true.

% 9d. Exactly one selected block (ensures uniqueness)
1 { selected(R0,C0) } :- true.
```

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Grid size
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) ) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Positions that fit a 3×3 block
%    (the block must stay inside the maximal coordinates)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row(R0)  :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) ),
            MaxR = #max { R2 : input(R2,_,_) ),
            R0 = 0..MaxR-2.                     % MaxR appears positively
col(C0)  :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) ),
            MaxC = #max { C2 : input(_,C2,_) ),
            C0 = 0..MaxC-2.                     % MaxC appears positively
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row(R0), col(C0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Uniform 3×3 block of colour C
%    – every non‑zero cell in the block equals C
%    – at least one cell equals C
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform(R0,C0,C) :-
    color(C), C>0,
    row(R0), col(C0),
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  “There exists a uniform block of colour C”
%    (C is the only variable in the head → safe)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_of_colour(C) :-
    uniform(R0,C0,C),                 % R0 and C0 are bound inside uniform
    row(R0), col(C0),
    color(C), C>0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Highest colour that occurs in a uniform block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
Cmax = #max { C : uniform_of_colour(C) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Smallest row (top‑most) that still gives a block of colour Cmax
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :-
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),          % Cmax already bound
    row(R0),                          % row(R0) gives a positive binding for R0
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), row(R1) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Smallest column (left‑most) for that row
% --------------------------------------------------------------
col_best(C0) :-
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),
    col(C0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), col(C1) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8.  The uniquely selected 3×3 block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0), col_best(C0), uniform(R0,C0,Cmax).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Output the nine cells of the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% non‑zero cell of the block
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    (input(R,C,V), V>0).

% implicit zero cell of the block
output(R,C,0) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    not input(R,C,_).

% exactly nine output atoms
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) }.

% exactly one selected block (guarantees uniqueness)
:- 1 { selected(R0,C0) }.

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

% Modulo arithmetic
even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

% Using a computed value in arithmetic
right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.
```

rewrite_details.system_prompt:
You are a Clingo/ASP syntax expert. Fix ONLY syntax errors — never change program logic or semantics.

# Common Clingo Syntax Errors

1. **No `aggregate/3`** — Use `N = #count { C : pred(C) }`, not `aggregate(N = #count, pred(C), N)`. The `aggregate/3` predicate is SWI-Prolog; it does not exist in Clingo.

2. **No `#mod`** — Use `\` for modulo: `C \ 2 = 0`, not `C #mod 2 = 0`. Clingo follows ISO Prolog arithmetic.

3. **Aggregate must be on the right-hand side** — Write `Var = #min { R : p(R) }`, not `#min { R : p(R) } = Var`. Clingo evaluates `Var = #aggr{...}`, not the reverse.

4. **`#const` names must be lowercase** — Write `#const max_dim = 30.`, not `#const MAX = 30.`. An uppercase token is parsed as a variable, which is invalid in a `#const` directive.

5. **No `exactly N` suffix on cardinality** — Write `1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).`, not `{ ... } exactly 1 :- cell(R,C).`. Clingo uses prefix/suffix integer bounds.

6. **Unsafe variables under negation** — Every variable must appear in at least one positive body literal. Add a positive binder before negating: `missing(R) :- row(R), col(C), not input(R,C,5).`, not `missing(R) :- col(C), not input(R,C,5).` (here `R` is unsafe).

7. **No predicate names in arithmetic** — `row_min` is a predicate, not an integer. Extract the value first: `border(R) :- row_min(Rmin), R = Rmin - 1.`, not `border(R) :- R = row_min - 1.`

8. **No top-level variable assignment** — Aggregates must appear in a rule with a head: `min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : p(R) }.`, not the standalone `MinR = #min { R : p(R) }.`

9. **No `succ/2`, `plus/3`, `msort/2`** — These are SWI-Prolog. Use arithmetic directly: `Next = N + 1`, `Sum = A + B`. There is no sort predicate in Clingo.

10. **`#count` body needs curly braces with colon** — Write `N = #count { X : pred(X) }`, not `N = #count(pred(X))`. The aggregate body uses `{ Vars : condition }` syntax.

11. **Aggregate variables must be grounded** — In `N = #count { R,C : input(R,C,_) }`, the grounding set must be finite. All variables in the tuple must appear positively in the condition.

12. **Range syntax uses `..` not `to`** — Write `R = 0..H-1`, not `R = 0 to H-1`. Range expressions always use double dot.

13. **No `assert/retract`** — Clingo programs are static. There is no assert or retract. All rules are declared upfront.

14. **Conditional literals need correct position** — `output(R,C,V) : input(R,C,V)` is a conditional literal, valid inside aggregates or choice heads. Not valid as a standalone rule body.

15. **Head disjunction uses `;`** — `a ; b :- body.` means either a or b (or both) must hold. This is different from `1 { a ; b } 1 :- body.` which is a choice with exactly one.

16. **No inline aggregate-to-aggregate comparison** — You cannot compare two aggregates directly in a rule body. Bind at least one to a variable first: `pivot(P) :- col(P), N = #count{R : row(R)}, N = #count{R : input(R,P,5)}.`, not `pivot(P) :- col(P), #count{R : input(R,P,5)} = #count{R : row(R)}.`. Alternatively, avoid the aggregate entirely with negation-as-failure: `bad(P) :- col(P), row(R), not input(R,P,5). pivot(P) :- col(P), not bad(P).`

# Correct Pattern Reference

```asp
% Computing grid dimensions
height(H) :- H = 1 + #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
width(W)  :- W = 1 + #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Domain for output cells (same size as input)
output_cell(R,C) :- height(H), width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% Choice rule (exactly one color per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Count non-zero cells in a row
nonzero_count(R, N) :- N = #count { C : input(R,C,V), V != 0 }, input(R,_,_).

% Minimum row containing color 5
min_row_5(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min { R : input(R,_,5) }.

% Safe negation (bind R and C positively first)
empty(R,C) :- output_cell(R,C), not input(R,C,_).

even_col(C) :- output_cell(_,C), C \ 2 = 0.

right_edge(R,C) :- width(W), output_cell(R,C), C = W - 1.

Program:
```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size (safe aggregates)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

row(R0) :- max_row_val(MaxR), R0 = 0..MaxR-2.
col(C0) :- max_col_val(MaxC), C0 = 0..MaxC-2.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row(R0), col(C0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Uniform 3×3 block of colour C
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform(R0,C0,C) :-
    color(C), C > 0,
    row(R0), col(C0),
    Zero = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V != 0, V != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeast = #count {
        V : input(R0+I, C0+J, V),
        I = 0..2, J = 0..2,
        V = C
    },
    AtLeast > 0.

uniform_of_colour(C) :-
    uniform(R0,C0,C), row(R0), col(C0), color(C), C > 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Highest colour occurring in a uniform block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
cmax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { C : uniform_of_colour(C) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Smallest row and column for that colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :-
    cmax(Cmax),
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),
    row(R0),
    R0 = #min { R1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), row(R1) }.

col_best(C0) :-
    cmax(Cmax),
    uniform_of_colour(Cmax),
    col(C0),
    C0 = #min { C1 : uniform_of_colour(Cmax), col(C1) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. The uniquely selected 3×3 block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0) :-
    row_best(R0), col_best(C0), uniform(R0,C0,Cmax).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Helper for block coordinates
% --------------------------------------------------------------
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Output the nine cells of the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,V) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    input(R,C,V),
    V > 0.

output(R,C,0) :-
    selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J),
    not input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Ensure exactly nine output atoms (one for each cell)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output_count(N) :- N = #count { R,C,V : output(R,C,V) }.
output_count(N), N = 9.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Ensure exactly one selected block (guarantees uniqueness)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected_count(S) :- S = #count { R0,C0 : selected(R0,C0) }.
selected_count(S), S = 1.
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Transformation rule:
% The output is a 3×3 block whose non‑zero cells all have the same colour C.
% Among all such blocks we choose the one with the highest colour C;
% if several blocks have that colour we pick the smallest row (topmost)
% and, among those, the smallest column (leftmost).  The output consists
% exactly of the nine cells of that block, copied from the input.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Input dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_col(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.
height(H)   :- max_row(MaxR),   H = MaxR + 1.
width(W)    :- max_col(MaxC),  W = MaxC + 1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. All possible positions of a 3×3 block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row0(R0)  :- max_row(Mr), R0 = 0..Mr-3.
col0(C0)  :- max_col(Mc), C0 = 0..Mc-3.
subgrid(R0,C0) :- row0(R0), col0(C0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Uniform 3×3 blocks (all non‑zero cells have the same colour C)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,C) :-
    subgrid(R0,C0),
    color(C), C != 0,
    Zero = #count {
        X : input(R0+i, C0+j, X),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2,
        X != 0, X != C
    },
    Zero = 0,
    AtLeastOne = #count {
        X : input(R0+i, C0+j, C),
        i = 0..2, j = 0..2
    },
    AtLeastOne > 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Helper predicate for uniform colours
% --------------------------------------------------------------
uniform_color(Col) :- uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,Col), color(Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Highest colour C in a uniform block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_color(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max { Col :
    uniform_color(Col), color(Col), Col != 0 }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Smallest row for Cmax
% --------------------------------------------------------------
row_best(R0) :- R0 = #min { R' :
    uniform_subgrid(R', C0', Cmax) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Smallest column for that row
% --------------------------------------------------------------
col_best(C0) :- uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,Cmax),
    C0 = #min { C' : uniform_subgrid(R0, C', Cmax) },
    row_best(R0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. The uniquely selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
selected(R0,C0) :- row_best(R0), col_best(C0), uniform_subgrid(R0,C0,Cmax).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Relative positions inside the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
pos(I,J) :- I = 0..2, J = 0..2.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Output cells – copy the nine cells of the selected block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- selected(R0,C0),
    R = R0 + I,
    C = C0 + J,
    pos(I,J).

output(R,C,V) :- output_cell(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. Exactly nine output cells (no extra cells)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- 9 { output(R,C,V) } .
```
